requirements.statement: The repository shall define a Mix project named PythonOntology with OTP application :python_ontology.
The project shall build a Python-native source ontology and analyzer that follows the broad architecture accepted in python_ontology.decision.initial_python_ontology_architecture.
The PythonOntology module shall expose stable file and project analysis entrypoints.
The PythonOntology module shall expose the OTP application name through app_name/0.
The Mix project shall include RDF.ex support for authored Turtle parsing and validation gates.
The Mix project shall include spec_led_ex as a dev/test-only dependency pinned to the shared repository ref.
The project shall include an ExUnit baseline that verifies the public namespace behavior.
The Mix project shall include Rustler support for the project-owned Tree-sitter parser NIF.
Builders shall produce RDF triples from extracted facts and builder context.
Pipeline stages shall accumulate structured diagnostics for recoverable issues.
Extractors shall produce structured Python facts from normalized syntax and analysis context.
Builders shall not parse source files or traverse raw Tree-sitter cursors.
Extractors shall not emit RDF triples directly.
Parser stages shall output normalized syntax data rather than extracted semantic facts or RDF triples.
Extractors and builders shall receive explicit context objects instead of relying on process-global state.
Builders shall use shared IRI generation helpers.
Extracted facts shall carry source evidence and confidence metadata.
Validation shall run after RDF graph building rather than inside parser or extractor stages.
RDF builders shall propagate fact confidence into generated triples using ontology classes or properties.
Generated facts shall support source_declared, statically_inferred, unresolved, and runtime_dependent confidence categories.
Dynamic constructs such as dynamic imports, getattr, setattr, monkey patching, decorators, and metaclasses shall preserve their uncertainty boundary.
The analyzer shall not execute analyzed Python code to increase confidence.
Confidence categories shall be queryable in generated RDF graphs.
Facts requiring Python runtime execution shall be marked runtime_dependent rather than source_declared or statically_inferred.
Facts extracted directly from parsed syntax shall default to source_declared confidence.
Statically inferred facts shall carry deterministic evidence references.
Relevant but unresolved facts shall be represented in the graph as queryable unresolved facts.
Each phase file shall list the ADRs, specs, and implementation surfaces it is intended to advance.
Each phase file shall end with a dedicated integration test section.
Each implementation wave shall include multiple phase files for its ADR-backed implementation area.
Each phase file shall use phase, section, task, and subtask hierarchy with a description at each phase, section, and task level.
Each implementation wave shall include an index that explains phase order, dependencies, and parallel work lanes for its ADR-backed area.
Parser, normalization, IRI identity, fact confidence, project scope, extractor/builder, validation, and initial slice planning shall each have a dedicated wave.
The first analysis slice shall extract calls, attributes, and simple call target syntax.
The first analysis slice shall extract classes and base class syntax.
The first analysis slice shall extract decorators and annotations as syntax-level facts.
The first analysis slice shall expose a public API or Mix task that writes Turtle output for a file or project.
The first analysis slice shall extract functions and methods.
The first analysis slice shall extract imports and aliases.
The first analysis slice shall extract modules and packages.
Runtime-dependent behavior, full type reasoning, framework semantics, dataflow, and dynamic import resolution shall remain out of scope for the first slice.
The first analysis slice shall extract parameters, defaults, varargs, kwargs, and keyword-only parameters.
The first analysis slice shall preserve source files and source locations for extracted entities.
The first analysis slice shall include tests for parser, extractor, builder, and output behavior.
Class, function, and method identities shall use lexical qualified names rather than runtime object identity or function arity.
Generated analyzed-code resource IRIs shall use a configurable base IRI.
Expressions, calls, imports, assignments, and source locations shall be identified by containing entity and source span.
Hash-based IRI fragments shall use documented canonical inputs.
Package and module identities shall be derived from discovered package roots, dotted names, and source file paths when needed.
Ontology vocabulary IRIs shall remain separate from generated analyzed-code resource IRIs.
Nested classes and nested functions shall include their lexical parent path in generated identities.
Static IRI generation shall not claim Python runtime object identity without runtime-specific evidence.
Ambiguous or repeated source definitions shall be disambiguated by occurrence index and source span.
Builders shall use a shared IRI helper instead of ad hoc string construction.
Source file identities shall use normalized repository-relative POSIX paths.
Normalization shall produce deterministic output for the same source text, parser version, and options.
Parser output consumed by extractors shall be represented as stable Elixir structs or maps.
Normalization shall not import or execute analyzed Python project code.
Normalization shall not emit RDF triples directly.
Normalized nodes shall retain raw Tree-sitter provenance including node type, named status, child order, and field names when available.
Normalized nodes shall preserve source file identity and available byte, line, and column spans.
Raw Tree-sitter nodes shall not be the stable internal model consumed by extractors.

The normalized model shall provide typed nodes for modules, imports, classes, functions, parameters, decorators, annotations, assignments, calls, attributes, subscripts, literals, identifiers, control flow, and comprehensions.
Unknown or unsupported parser nodes shall be preserved as generic normalized nodes instead of being silently discarded.
Initial Turtle files shall contain parseable ontology declarations, metadata, and representative starter classes or properties for their layer.
The ontology shall distinguish source-declared facts, statically inferred facts, unresolved facts, and runtime-dependent dynamic facts.
The repository shall store authored Python ontology Turtle files under priv/ontologies.
Ontology layer imports shall flow from foundational source concepts toward structure, typing, runtime, evolution, and shapes without circular imports.
The ontology shall be split into python-core, python-structure, python-typing, python-runtime, python-evolution, and python-shapes Turtle files.
Each Turtle ontology layer shall use a stable namespace rooted at https://w3id.org/python-code/.
The ontology layers shall model Python-native source concepts rather than directly copying Elixir ontology concepts.
The shapes ontology shall declare starter SHACL shapes for first-slice modules, functions, parameters, and source locations.
Parser implementations shall conform to an explicit adapter boundary so alternate adapters can be introduced without changing extractors.
Parser output shall preserve Tree-sitter concrete syntax tree structure and node type names before semantic extraction.
The default parser adapter shall be owned by the Elixir project through a Tree-sitter binding or project-owned NIF.
Parser failures and Tree-sitter error nodes shall be represented as structured parse errors instead of unclassified runtime failures.
The parser layer shall not emit RDF triples directly; RDF generation belongs to extractors and builders.
The parser shall parse Python source without importing or executing the analyzed project code.
The default parser path shall not require an external Python process, embedded CPython, Pythonx, or analyzed project imports.
Parser output shall be normalized into Elixir data structures before extractor logic consumes it.
Parser output shall report Tree-sitter grammar or parser version information when available.
CPython ast, LibCST, or Pythonx may be introduced only as optional parser adapters that conform to the same parser contract and are not the default bridge.
Parser output shall preserve source file identity and available line and column span information.
The first parser implementation shall use Tree-sitter's Python grammar as the syntax authority.
Include and exclude file selection shall be configurable with glob patterns.
File traversal order shall be deterministic.
Project analysis shall exclude common virtualenv, cache, build, distribution, dependency, and VCS directories by default.
Project analysis shall include .py files by default.
Project analysis shall include .pyi stub files by default.
Project analysis shall represent namespace package directories that lack __init__.py but belong to importable package trees.
Project analysis shall not traverse installed dependencies by default.
Project analysis shall recognize regular Python packages that contain __init__.py files.
Project roots shall be detected from explicit paths, pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, setup.py, or git roots.
The analyzer shall support both single-file analysis and project-root analysis.
Test files shall be included by default and marked as test scope.
User-facing gates shall have command verifications once implementation exists.
Extractor and builder behavior shall be covered by focused tests and integration tests.
Validation shall not execute analyzed Python project code.
OWL ontology declarations shall define vocabulary and semantic relationships without replacing local closed-world checks.
Parser and normalization behavior shall be covered by focused tests.
SHACL shall validate generated RDF graphs for closed-world completeness and consistency.
SpecLed shall validate current-truth specs, ADRs, and branch coverage.
Authored Turtle ontology files shall be parseable RDF/Turtle.
Generated graph validation shall run after RDF graph building.
Validation shall produce structured reports suitable for CLI output and tests.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that tells agents to start a session with mix spec.prime before editing current truth.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.
When indexing and validation run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.

given: normalized syntax contains an import statement
then: it emits an import fact with source evidence, and a builder later turns that fact into RDF
the pipeline records a diagnostic without forcing builders to parse source text
the fact is source_declared and carries source evidence
the generated fact remains unresolved or runtime_dependent and queryable

given: a Python module uses dynamic import or decorator behavior that cannot be resolved statically
then: the behavior remains unresolved or runtime-dependent instead of being over-inferred
when: the first analysis slice processes the module

given: a Python module defines imports, a class, and a function
then: the output graph includes module, import, class, function, parameter, and source location resources
when: the first analysis slice processes the module

given: a function named parse exists in package/module analyzer/parser.py
then: the function IRI is derived from the configured base IRI, normalized path or module identity, and lexical function path
when: builders generate RDF resources for the function

given: a Python module defines the same class name twice
then: occurrence and source span data disambiguate the generated IRIs
when: builders generate resources for both definitions

given: Tree-sitter returns a Python function definition node
then: the output contains a typed function node with parser provenance and source spans
when: the normalization layer processes the parse tree

given: Tree-sitter returns a node not yet understood by PythonOntology
then: the output keeps the node as a generic normalized node with its raw type and children
when: the normalization layer processes the parse tree

given: a Python source file contains imports or top-level code with side effects
then: the parser reads syntax through Tree-sitter without importing or executing the analyzed project code

given: a Python source file contains invalid syntax
then: Elixir receives structured Tree-sitter error information with location information when available
when: the default parser adapter analyzes the file

given: a Python source file can be parsed by Tree-sitter's Python grammar
then: Elixir receives a successful normalized parse payload with source identity, parser version data when available, and Tree-sitter node data
when: the default parser adapter analyzes the file

given: a repository contains pyproject.toml, src/my_pkg/__init__.py, src/my_pkg/app.py, and .venv/lib/site-packages/pkg.py
then: the analyzer includes src/my_pkg files and excludes .venv files
when: project analysis runs with default options

given: a project contains package interface stubs in .pyi files
then: the analyzer includes the stub files and marks them as source artifacts
when: project analysis runs with default options

given: a generated RDF graph contains a function resource without a qualified name
then: the validation report identifies the missing required property

given: an authored ontology Turtle file contains invalid Turtle syntax
then: the gate fails before graph analysis proceeds

meta.summary: Separation of normalized syntax, extracted facts, RDF builders, and validation.
Confidence categories for source-declared, inferred, unresolved, and runtime-dependent Python facts.
ADR-specific phased implementation waves for parser, normalization, identity, confidence, project scope, builders, validation, and the first analysis slice.
First implementation slice for ordinary Python modules, classes, functions, imports, calls, and source locations.
Deterministic IRI strategy for analyzed Python code resources.
Stable normalized Elixir representation of parsed Python syntax.
Layered Turtle ontology files for Python source-code knowledge graphs.
Python-native source ontology and analyzer for Python projects.
Tree-sitter parser boundary from Python source text to normalized Elixir data.
Python project discovery, include/exclude policy, and file traversal scope.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs and generated Spec Led state.
Layered validation strategy for PythonOntology artifacts and generated graphs.
